data.title: Correction: Mentice to Present the Company's Year-End Q4 Report 2024 via Webcast
data.description:
Updated Time Webcast Conference Call

Mentice AB (STO: MNTC) announces that the company will present its Year-End Q4-2024 interim report for the period October – December via a webcast conference call on February 13, 2025, at 11:00 CET.

During the webcast, CEO Frans Venker and CFO Ulrika Drotz will provide insights into the company's financial performance and strategic developments. The presentation will be followed by a Q&A session, moderated by Carnegie analyst Rikard Engberg.

The presentation will be held in English, and participants are encouraged to register in advance.

For more information on how to join the webcast and participate, please visit https://investor.mentice.com/.

data.title: AstraZeneca Expects Strong Growth Momentum to Continue in 'Catalyst-Rich' 2025
data.description:
AstraZeneca expects to continue its strong growth momentum in 2025, touting a "catalyst-rich" year marked by late-stage readouts for new medicines and potential indication expansions for existing drugs.

The Anglo-Swedish pharmaceutical giant is set to release the first phase 3 data for seven new molecular entities in 2025, including cancer drugs camizestrant and ceralasertib, according to a Thursday release. Late-stage clinical results will also be available for hypertension medicine baxdrostat, and rare disease therapies anselamimab, efzimfotase alfa, eneboparatide and gefurulimab.

For additional indications, AstraZeneca will announce late-stage readouts for Datroway in lung and breast cancer, Enhertu in HER2+ breast cancer, and Imfinzi in bladder cancer. Outside of oncology, the company is due to provide phase 3 results for Breztri and Fasenra in severe asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, respectively.

"This year marks the beginning of an unprecedented, catalyst-rich period for our company, an important step on our Ambition 2030 journey to deliver $80 billion Total Revenue by the end of the decade," AstraZeneca Chief Executive Officer Pascal Soriot said.

On the financial side, the company forecasts a high single-digit percentage increase in total revenue for 2025, with core EPS growth expected at a low double-digit percentage. Both guidance are at constant exchange rates.

In 2024, the drugmaker's attributable profit reached $7.04 billion, up from $5.96 billion a year ago. Total revenue also climbed to $54.07 billion from $45.81 billion. AstraZeneca said its revenue growth was mainly driven by its oncology segment, followed by respiratory and immunology, and cardiovascular, renal and metabolism therapeutic areas.

data.title: Rayitek Hi-Tech Film Receives 21-Million-Yuan Government Subsidy
data.description:
Rayitek Hi-Tech Film subsidiary Shenzhen Ruihuatai Applied Materials Technology received a government subsidy of 21.2 million yuan, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the thermal control polyimide and electronic polyimide film producer rose 3% Thursday.

data.title: Ugandan shilling flat ahead of central bank rate decision
data.description:
The Ugandan shilling was flat on Thursday ahead of a central bank rate decision, LSEG data showed.

At 0801 GMT commercial banks quoted the shilling at 3,675/3,685, the same level as Wednesday's close.

data.title: Pernod Ricard's Guidance Cut Looks Realistic — Market Talk
data.description: Pernod Ricard's guidance cut means the company is adopting a more realistic framework for its outlook that could pave the way for a share recovery down the line, Jefferies's Edward Mundy and Andrei Andon-Ionita say. The maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Scotch whisky now forecasts a fall in organic sales for fiscal 2025, against its previous expectations of a return to growth. Consensus expectations had already moved below the company's guidance and shares in Pernod Ricard had been weak on concerns about its performance in China, the analysts say in a research note. The guidance cut should reduce the risk for Pernod Ricard's full-year earnings disappointing, Jefferies says. Shares rise 2.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0829 GMT - Pernod Ricard's guidance cut means the company is adopting a more realistic framework for its outlook that could pave the way for a share recovery down the line, Jefferies's Edward Mundy and Andrei Andon-Ionita say. The maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Scotch whisky now forecasts a fall in organic sales for fiscal 2025, against its previous expectations of a return to growth. Consensus expectations had already moved below the company's guidance and shares in Pernod Ricard had been weak on concerns about its performance in China, the analysts say in a research note. The guidance cut should reduce the risk for Pernod Ricard's full-year earnings disappointing, Jefferies says. Shares rise 2.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0826 GMT - Watches of Switzerland Group's maintained guidance will likely be reassuring, Shore Capital's David Hughes writes. The London-based luxury watch retailer's third-quarter update shows encouraging signs for sales, Hughes says in a note. These include the positive performance of its businesses, improving market conditions in U.K. and continued momentum in the U.S. "Given the worsening views on the economic outlook of the U.K., and a number of recent profit downgrades from retailers following a softer seasonal period, a report of good trading and maintained fiscal 2025 guidance should reassure," he adds. Shares are down 1.8% at 544.50 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0822 GMT - Max Financial Services' life-insurance unit seems well placed to achieve its FY 2025 guidance, Nomura analysts say in a research report. For the unit, management guides for 20% annual-premium-equivalent growth and high-single-digit growth for value of new business in FY 2025, the analysts note. Management appears reasonably confident that the unit can post a VNB margin of 23%-24% in FY 2025, the analysts say. With the effects of India's surrender value regulations for the insurance industry now mostly factored in, the company appears well on course to deliver on the guidance, the analysts add. Nomura upgrades the stock to buy from neutral and raises the target price to INR1,300.00 from INR1,250.00. Shares are 1.0% lower at INR1,107.60. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0810 GMT - Hannover Re's confirmation of its guidance for 2025 is positive in light of the Los Angeles wildfires, which have been a clear drag on the reinsurance sector year to date, analysts at JPMorgan say. The German reinsurer didn't provide an estimate on the impact of the California fires, but it did reaffirm its previous guidance for net profit of 2.4 billion euros this year. It also confirmed earnings for last year at 2.3 billion euros, in line with previous guidance and said it achieved premium growth of 7.6% at its January treaty renewals with pricing reduced 2.1% on a risk-adjusted basis. "Price reductions for Hannover Re take profitability back to 2023 levels which was a strong baseline in our view," JPM says. Shares rise 0.3%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0803 GMT - U.S. tariffs will likely have a minimal impact on software companies, while hardware companies will face a bigger hit, Janus Henderson Investors says in a commentary. Software isn't reliant on physical asset production and has a geographical distributed workforce, whereas hardware is often manufactured abroad and relies on components such as semiconductor chips and wafers. The components are mainly produced in Asia-Pacific and then shipped to Mexico for assembly to take advantage of lower labor costs, the asset manager adds. "The impact of the recently announced tariffs would therefore be most pronounced in the assembly phase." Janus Henderson expects the impact on large end-market manufacturers like Dell and HP to be more acute than their suppliers. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - China's equity markets could have a tough year regardless of how the trade tensions play out, Capital Economics analyst Thomas Mathews says in a research note. Investors may be overly optimistic about the likelihood of a reprieve on tariffs, the analyst says, and tariffs are not the only reason to be downbeat. The prospects of further fiscal stimulus, which investors have put high hopes on, are uncertain, he says. "A separate problem is that Chinese companies have struggled for a long time to generate sustained growth in earnings per share," he adds. Therefore, there's play of room for conditions in China's markets to deteriorate if the tariffs remain in place, he adds.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher amid receding concerns of trade friction between the U.S. and its trading partners. Brokerage and electronics stocks led the gains. Nomura Holdings rose 3.8% after its nine-month net profit more than doubled. Renesas Electronics surged 13% after its 4Q results. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.6% to 39066.53. USD/JPY is at 152.64, compared with 152.60 as of Wednesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. Investors are closely watching for developments regarding U.S. tariffs. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2 basis points to 1.260%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0724 GMT - Chinese shares ended higher, with the Shanghai Composite Index rising 1.3% to 3270.66. The Shenzhen Composite Index added 2.3% to 1964.55 and the ChiNext Price Index gained 2.8% to 2120.75. Investors are focusing on developments regarding tariffs. Among major stocks, BYD shares rose 10% on high expectations for its upcoming smart car press conference. SMIC added 3.0% and Great Wall Motor gained 2.1%. Decliners included Beijing Kingsoft Office Software, which was 1.2% lower, and China Construction Bank, which was 0.5% lower. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 73 points higher, or 0.9%, according to IG. The index closed 52 points higher, or 0.6%, to 8523 points Wednesday. Market sentiment across Europe and the U.S. has improved on waning trade tensions with President Trump, Swissquote Bank analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. Investors are also looking ahead to the latest policy decision from the Bank of England at 1200 GMT. The BOE is expected to lower interest rates by 25 basis points as U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves's growth plans are "clouded by the country's restricted finances and the economy calls for BOE support," she says.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0719 GMT - Societe Generale's better-than-expected results and guidance for 2025 that points to more cost controls should provide confidence that its 2026 targets are in reach, RBC Capital Markets' Anke Reingen and Matthew Russell say in a research note. The French lender delivered a good set of results, the analysts say. An increase to its payout ratio to half of net profit from 2024 onward isn't totally surprising, RBC says. Moreover, Societe Generale is targeting a return on tangible equity--a key profitability metric--of more than 8% in 2025 and reiterated its guidance of a return of 9%-10% next year. This should give confidence on its outlook, given that consensus expectations are close to the low end of its 2025 guidance but remain below Societe Generale's 2026 targets, RBC says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi rose 1.1% to close at 2536.75, extending gains for a third consecutive session. Construction, internet and semiconductor stocks advanced. Wall Street's overnight gains helped buoy sentiment. Foreign and institutional investors were net buyers. Mobile internet company Kakao rose 4.9%, extending its rally after agreeing earlier this week with OpenAI to develop artificial-intelligence products. Daewoo Engineering & Construction gained 4.2% after its 4Q earnings beat. Memory-chip maker SK Hynix added 2.4%. Meanwhile, KB Financial Group fell 6.7% after its 4Q earnings miss. USD/KRW settled 0.2% higher at 1,447.70 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was down 4.2 bps at 2.776%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0549 GMT - Global Power Synergy's earnings may be impacted by a potential electricity tariff cut in Thailand, CGS International's Amornrat Cheevavichawalkul says in a research report, as the brokerage lowers its target price to THB32.50 from THB43.50. The Energy Regulatory Commission of Thailand has proposed a reduction in electricity tariff to THB3.98/unit, versus the current THB4.15/unit, by removing the adder tariff and feed-in-tariff from the electricity cost structure, the analyst notes. The brokerage estimates that every THB1/unit reduction in the electricity tariff should lead to a 12.8% decline in the Thai power company's 2025 EPS. The brokerage maintains its hold rating as concerns over potentially lower tariffs should mostly be priced in. Shares are 5.9% lower at THB28.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Aurubis rises after slight Q1 earnings beat, investor plans to hike stake
data.description:
** Aurubis jumps 4% after it reported a rise in Q1 operating pretax profit, beating analysts expectations and plans of a stake increase by one of its major shareholders

** Europe's largest copper producer posted a 17% rise in operating earnings before taxes (EBT) to 130 mln euros ($134.8 mln), beating analysts' expectations of 126 mln euros

** Aurubis said EBT in its multimetal recycling segment was hit by ramp-up costs in its Richmond plant in Georgia

** "Some for the bulls and some for the bears," a local trader said, adding they expected a first positive reaction on the better Q1 EBT number

** German investor Dirk Rossmann said he plans to further increase his stake in Aurubis, which should support the share price in the short term, an analyst at Baader Bank said

($1 = 0.9641 euros)

data.title: 'Trying to paint over the hole in the leaky FCF': Jefferies downgrades Akzo
data.description:
** Jefferies cuts Akzo Nobel to "hold" from "buy" on lack of "meaningful" FCF generation in 2025, saying the company is "trying to paint over the hole in the leaky FCF"

** It also points to lack of EBIT improvement in last week's FY report

** It notes that this pares hopes of additional shareholder returns (post-India divestment)

** The brokerage says Akzo will need to improve operating results or close the valuation gap with peers, which doesn't seem apparent in 2025

** Otherwise, Akzo might again become a bid target, Jefferies says

** The broker sees Akzo's interest in a potential consolidation of the Coatings assets as an overhang on the investment case

** It adds that the net debt guidance implies a limited or no absolute deleveraging in 2025

** Shares are down 1.4%

** Out of 21 analysts that cover Akzo Nobel, 12 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy," six rate "hold" and three rate it "strong sell" or "sell"

($1 = 0.9626 euros)

data.title: Jet2 Repurchases GBP33 Million Convertible Bonds due 2026
data.description:
Jet2 repurchased 33 million pounds sterling of its 337.4 million-pound 1.625% guaranteed convertible bonds due 2026 for cancellation.

Following the repurchase, 304.4 million pounds of the bonds remain outstanding, the airline company said Thursday.

data.title: UAE's Aman Unaware of Reason for Unusual Trading Activity
data.description:
Dubai Islamic Insurance & Reinsurance (DFM:AMAN), d/b/a Aman, on Thursday said it is unaware of any recent developments that could impact its stock.

The Emirati insurance company made the statement addressed to the Dubai Financial Market after its share price and volumes saw an "unusual" rise.

The stock up was up 6.43% at midday trading.

data.title: Arabica Futures Scale New Peak
data.description:
Arabica coffee futures continued to soar, breaching $4 per pound, a new record high since 1970s, fueled by speculative buying and lingering concerns over tight supplies.

Arabica prices have been supported by an expected significant output decline in top producer Brazil in the upcoming season, following an extended dry spell in 2024 that hit its latest harvest. In its first estimate for 2025-2026, Brazil's National Supply Company projected coffee production to fall by 4.4% to a three-year low of 51.81 million bags, even though the weather is more favorable.

data.title: Hang Seng Rallies to Close Near 2-Month Peak
data.description:
The Hang Seng surged 294 points or 1.4% to end at 20,892 on Thursday, reversing weakness in the prior session amid a rise in US futures after Wall Street's major indexes saw back-to-back gains Wednesday.

Markets revisited their highest level in almost two months, amid mounting optimism around China's AI sector following a breakthrough from the start-up DeepSeek.

The tech index climbed 2.6%, buoyed by robust gains from Sunny Optical Tech. (10.4%), Semiconductor Manufacturing (6.3%), and Hua Hong Semiconductor (7.5%).

BYD Co. soared near 20% as it plans to hold an intelligent strategy launch event next week and allow people to experience advanced intelligent driving.

Consumers and property also booked solid gains, rising by 1.9% and 1.7%, respectively.

Limiting further gains was vigilance ahead of Friday’s NFP data in the US, and January CPI and PPI data in China next week.

Other top movers included Geely Auto (5.0%), Wuxi Biologics (4.7%), SenseTime Group (4.2%), and KE Hlds. (3.7%).

data.description:
Carnegie has published a new flash comment on Pricer following recent news flow.

To access the full report and subscribe to future updates, visit https://www.carnegie.se/en/commissioned-research/pricer/

data.title: Market Chatter: Trump's Removal of Tariff Exemptions for Smaller Items Could Hurt China Economy, Nomura Says
data.description:
An order by the administration of US President Donald Trump to remove tariff exemptions on small packages and subject them to normal Customs duties would hurt Chinese economic growth in 2025, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing economists at Nomura.

The removal of the tariff exemptions on items worth below $800 would impact tens of billions of dollars of trade, slash Chinese export growth by 1.3 percentage points, and clip China's gross domestic product growth by 0.2 percentage points, the report said, citing the note.

In 2024, e-commerce sites such as Shein and Temu shipped $46 billion worth of small parcels to the US, the report said.

China's cross-Pacific trade reached $23 billion in 2024, Bloomberg said, citing data from the country's General Administration of Customs.

The number could be bigger as companies ship larger quantities of items to Mexico before breaking them down into smaller packages to enter the US, according to Bloomberg.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: NTT DATA Group's Nine-Month Profit Jumps 15%
data.description:
NTT DATA Group's profit attributable to shareholders increased by 15.2% to 96.5 billion yen in the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 83.74 billion yen a year ago.

The information technology firm's net income per share rose to 68.80 yen from 59.73 yen a year ago, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

Net sales grew 7.3% to 3.408 trillion yen from 3.176 trillion yen a year earlier.

For the fiscal year ending March 31, the company forecasts attributable profit to grow 2.3% to 137.00 billion yen, with net income per share at 97.72 yen. Net sales are projected to increase by 1.4% to 4.43 trillion yen.

The company expects to pay a final dividend of 12.5 yen per share.

data.title: IMI Reports Unauthorized System Access in Cyber Security Incident
data.description:
IMI reported a cyber security incident involving unauthorized access to its systems.

The specialist engineering company said in a Thursday filing that it has engaged external cyber security experts to investigate and contain the incident after it became aware of the unauthorized access.

data.title: London-listed BBGI surges on its acquisition by BCI in $1.32 billion deal
data.description:
** Shares of BBGI Global Infrastructure S.A. surge as much as 19.9% to 146p, their highest since June 2023

** Stock top gainer on FTSE 250 (.FTMC) index

** Canada's British Columbia Investment Management Corp will buy the Luxembourg-based investment firm in a deal valuing the company at 1.06 billion pounds ($1.32 billion), the companies said

** BBGI shareholders will get 147.5p-per-share in cash, a premium of 21% to the stock's closing price on Wednesday

** Stock had fallen 12% in 2024

data.title: United Parcel Service Lifts Dividend to $1.64 a Share, Payable March 6 to Shareholders as of Feb. 18
ING shares slide after missing Q4 expectations, tempering 2025 outlook
data.description:
** Shares in the largest Dutch bank ING are trading down as much as 3.7% to a three-week low after reporting its Q4 and FY 2024 results

** JPM analysts call Q4 results "weak", pointing to higher costs and lower investment income more than offsetting consensus beats from net interest income and fees income

** "We would expect EPS downgrades, driven by higher cost guidance with the new revenue guidance also implying lower NII versus higher other income," JPM said in a note regarding ING'’s 2025 outlook

** Deutsche Bank calls the Q4 results "ok to slightly soft", citing a miss on pre-provision profit and calling the NII beat "not ideal"

** As of 0803 GMT, shares of ING are down 3.74%

data.title: Greencore Target Raised to 225p From 195p by Deutsche Bank
